channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Fraud Suspect Rondozai Up for Kidnapping
item.description: By Crime and Courts Reporter | A Harare man is set to stand trial in two separate criminal cases at... Continue reading "Fraud Suspect Rondozai Up for Kidnapping" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Selling beer on credit is illegal, Zimbabwe police warn @bulawayo24
item.description: Beer outlets operating "tab" systems or allowing customers to drink now and pay later are breaking the law and risk severe penalties, including the loss of their operating licences, authorities have w... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Kenya denies awarding $1.2-billion contract to Chivayo @bulawayo24 @wicknellchivayo
item.description: The Kenyan government has dismissed reports linking a company associated with controversial Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo to the planned expansion of Nairobi’s Jomo Kenyatta International Ai... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Scottland players set for record US$10,000 bonuses @bulawayo24
item.description: Players at Scottland FC are set to receive what is believed to be the biggest single cash bonus ever awarded to players in the Castle Lager Premier Soccer League after club president Pedzisayi "Scott"... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Oil, gas project to slash fuel import bill @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe is moving closer to reducing its annual fuel import bill of more than US$1 billion as exploration activities in the Cabora Bassa Basin continue to advance, strengthening hopes for improved en... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Businesses must pay compliance fees to councils @bulawayo24
item.description: The Bulawayo City Council (BCC) has raised concern over the growing number of businesses operating without licences and registration certificates, warning that the trend is depriving the local authori... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: New law on over­load­ing on the cards @bulawayo24
item.description: The Government is drafting new legislation aimed at curbing the dangerous practice of overloading schoolchildren in public transport vehicles following the tragic Gweru kombi fire that claimed the liv... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Council loses bid to halt US$250K workers’ pay battle @bulawayo24
item.description: The High Court in Bulawayo has dismissed an application by Vungu Rural District Council (RDC) to rescind a default judgment that allows workers to execute against council assets in a long-running disp... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Tombstones intercepted in US$9m border raids @bulawayo24
item.description: The Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (Zimra) says its technology-driven anti-smuggling campaign has generated more than US$9 million in revenue collections while intercepting a wide range of contraband good... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe platinum mines hit crisis point @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe’s platinum mining industry says it is owed more than US$228 million by the government in unpaid export proceeds, a situation miners warn is straining cash flows, delaying investment projects... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Three Women Rape Chitungwiza Man, Steal Cash
item.description: By A Correspondent| A 45-year-old man from Chitungwiza was allegedly sexually assaulted by three women on Sunday after they reportedly... Continue reading "Three Women Rape Chitungwiza Man, Steal Cash" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: REPLY TO THE SECOND READING DEBATE CONSTITUTION OF ZIMBABWE AMENDMENT BILL (NO. 3), 2026
item.description: By Ziyambi Ziyambi Introduction Mr. Speaker Sir, I rise to reply to the Second Reading debate on the Constitution of Zimbabwe (Amendment No.3) H.B.1. Bill, 2026. Before doing so, may I thank Honourable Members for their spirited and constructive engagement of the Bill. Over seven sitting days, members from every province and both sides of [...] The post REPLY TO THE SECOND READING DEBATE CONSTITUTION OF ZIMBABWE AMENDMENT BILL (NO. 3), 2026 appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwe platinum producers owed $228 million in unpaid export earnings
item.description: The country requires all exporters to convert 30% of their proceeds into local currency, but the government is frequently behind in paying out. (via Mining) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Mining

item.title: 20 Arrested as Harare Launches Fresh Crackdown on Littering @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: 20 Arrested as Harare Launches Fresh Crackdown on Littering Twenty people were arrested in Harare’s central business district on Wednesday as city authorities intensified efforts to tackle littering and improve cleanliness in the capital. The operation was carried out jointly by the Harare City Council, the Environmental Management Agency (EMA) and the Zimbabwe Republic Police [...] The post 20 Arrested as Harare Launches Fresh Crackdown on Littering appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
